configuration-properties.doc:name: Configuration Properties
configuration-properties.doc:description: Loads Salesforce connection settings (host, port, basePath) from config.yaml resource file

http:listener-config.name: HTTP_Listener_config
http:listener-config.doc:name: HTTP Listener Config
http:listener-config.doc:description: HTTP listener configuration on host 0.0.0.0 port 8081 to receive incoming token requests

http:listener-connection.doc:description: Binds the HTTP listener to all network interfaces on port 8081 to accept incoming connections

http:request-config.name: Salesforce_OAuth_Request_Config
http:request-config.doc:name: Salesforce OAuth Request Config
http:request-config.doc:description: HTTPS request configuration targeting the Salesforce OAuth2 token endpoint at your Salesforce instance URL

http:request-connection.doc:description: Establishes an HTTPS connection to the Salesforce OAuth2 endpoint using host and port from config.yaml

flow.name: get-salesforce-access-token-flow
flow.doc:name: Get Salesforce Access Token Flow
flow.doc:description: Exposes POST /token; accepts clientId and clientSecret in the JSON request body and retrieves a Salesforce OAuth2 access token using client credentials grant

http:listener.doc:name: POST /token
http:listener.doc:description: Receives incoming POST requests on /token with JSON body containing clientId and clientSecret to trigger Salesforce OAuth2 token retrieval

logger.message: Requesting Salesforce OAuth2 access token using client credentials flow
logger.doc:name: Log token request start
logger.doc:description: Logs the initiation of the Salesforce OAuth2 token request before calling the token endpoint

ee:transform.doc:name: Build OAuth2 Request Body
ee:transform.doc:description: Reads grant_type from config.yaml and clientId and clientSecret from the POST request body; builds a URL-encoded form body for the Salesforce OAuth2 token request

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload as a URL-encoded form body for the Salesforce token request

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Constructs grant_type, client_id, and client_secret as URL-encoded form data for the Salesforce OAuth2 token request

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/x-www-form-urlencoded --- { grant_type: p("salesforce.grantType"), client_id: payload.clientId, client_secret: payload.clientSecret }

http:request.doc:name: POST to Salesforce OAuth2 Token Endpoint
http:request.doc:description: Posts client credentials as URL-encoded form data to Salesforce /services/oauth2/token to obtain an access token

ee:transform.doc:name: Extract Access Token from Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Extracts access_token, token_type, and instance_url from the Salesforce OAuth2 JSON response and returns them as the HTTP response payload

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload extracted from the Salesforce OAuth2 JSON response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Extracts access_token, token_type, and instance_url from the Salesforce OAuth2 response into a JSON object

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { access_token: payload.access_token, token_type: payload.token_type, instance_url: payload.instance_url }

os:store.doc:name: Store Access Token in Object Store
os:store.doc:description: Stores the Salesforce access token in the default Mule in-memory Object Store. Works on CH1, CH2, on-prem, and RTF — all flows in the same app instance share this store.

logger.message: #['Salesforce access token stored in Object Store. Token type: ' ++ (payload.token_type default 'unknown') ++ '. Key: salesforce_access_token.']
logger.doc:name: Log token stored in Object Store
logger.doc:description: Logs that the Salesforce OAuth2 access token was successfully stored in the Object Store under key salesforce_access_token

error-handler.doc:description: Handles HTTP errors from the Salesforce OAuth2 token endpoint including 401 Unauthorized and connectivity failures

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle Unauthorized
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles 401 Unauthorized from Salesforce; typically caused by invalid client_id or client_secret in the request body

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 401 Error Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON error body explaining that the Salesforce credentials are invalid

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the 401 Unauthorized error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON error body with error type, message, and error details for the 401 Unauthorized response

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Unauthorized", message: "Failed to obtain Salesforce access token. Verify the clientId and clientSecret in the request body.", details: error.description }

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle Connectivity Error
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles connectivity failures when reaching the Salesforce OAuth2 endpoint; check the host in config.yaml

ee:transform.doc:name: Build Connectivity Error Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON error body for connectivity failures to the Salesforce OAuth2 endpoint

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the connectivity error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON error body describing the Salesforce OAuth2 connectivity failure with error details

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Connectivity Error", message: "Unable to connect to Salesforce OAuth2 endpoint. Check the Salesforce host in config.yaml.", details: error.description }

flow.name: get-cached-salesforce-token-flow
flow.doc:name: Get Cached Salesforce Token Flow
flow.doc:description: Exposes GET /token/cached; retrieves the Salesforce access token from the default Object Store and returns it as a JSON response

http:listener.doc:name: GET /token/cached
http:listener.doc:description: Receives incoming GET requests on /token/cached to retrieve the cached Salesforce access token from the Object Store

try.doc:name: Try Retrieve Cached Token
try.doc:description: Attempts to retrieve the cached Salesforce access token; handles OS:KEY_NOT_FOUND gracefully when no token has been stored yet

os:retrieve.doc:name: Retrieve Access Token from Object Store
os:retrieve.doc:description: Retrieves the value stored under key 'salesforce_access_token' from the default Object Store into vars.accessToken

on-error-continue.doc:name: Handle Key Not Found
on-error-continue.doc:description: Handles the case when no token has been stored yet; sets accessToken to null so the choice router returns a 404-style response

set-variable.doc:name: Set Null Token
set-variable.doc:description: Sets accessToken to null when the key does not exist in the Object Store

choice.doc:name: Check Token Exists
choice.doc:description: Routes based on whether a cached Salesforce access token was found in the Object Store

when.doc:name: Token Found
when.doc:description: Returns the cached access token as JSON when it exists in the Object Store

ee:transform.doc:name: Build Token Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Builds a JSON response containing the cached Salesforce access token retrieved from the Object Store

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload as a JSON object with the access_token field

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Creates a JSON payload with the access_token value retrieved from the Object Store

otherwise.doc:name: Token Not Found
otherwise.doc:description: Returns a 404-style JSON error when no cached token exists in the Object Store

ee:transform.doc:name: Build Token Not Found Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Builds a JSON error response indicating no Salesforce access token is cached; caller should invoke POST /token first

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload as a JSON error object

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Creates a JSON error payload indicating the token is not present in the Object Store

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Token Not Found", message: "No Salesforce access token found in Object Store. Call POST /token first." }

error-handler.doc:description: Handles unexpected errors when retrieving the cached access token from the Object Store

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle Retrieval Error
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Catches any unexpected error during Object Store token retrieval and returns a structured JSON error response

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 500 Error Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON 500 error body for unexpected errors during cached token retrieval

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the 500 Internal Server Error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON 500 error body for unexpected failures retrieving the cached Salesforce token

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Internal Server Error", message: "An unexpected error occurred while retrieving the cached access token.", details: error.description }

flow.name: extract-data-flow
flow.doc:name: Extract Data Flow
flow.doc:description: Accepts POST /extract-data with a multipart file; reads the Salesforce access token from the Object Store, Base64-encodes the file, and calls Flow IDP to extract document data

http:listener.doc:name: POST /extract-data
http:listener.doc:description: Receives incoming POST requests on /extract-data with a multipart/form-data body containing a file part named 'file'

ee:transform.doc:name: Extract and Base64-encode File
ee:transform.doc:description: Reads the 'file' part from the multipart payload, extracts MIME type and filename, and encodes the binary content as a Base64 string

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload containing the Base64-encoded file data from the multipart request

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Extracts filename, MIME type, and Base64-encoded binary content from the multipart file part into a JSON object

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 import toBase64 from dw::core::Binaries output application/json var filePart = payload.parts.file var fileName = filePart.headers.'Content-Disposition'.filename default "attachment" --- { fileName: fileName, mimeType: filePart.headers.'Content-Type', base64Content: toBase64(filePart.content as Binary) }

logger.message: #['File encoded. MIME type: ' ++ (payload.mimeType default 'unknown') ++ ', fileName: ' ++ (payload.fileName default 'unknown')]
logger.doc:name: Log File Extracted
logger.doc:description: Logs the MIME type and filename of the uploaded file after Base64 encoding

set-variable.doc:name: Store Base64 Content
set-variable.doc:description: Saves the Base64-encoded file content to a variable before os:retrieve replaces the payload

set-variable.doc:name: Store MIME Type
set-variable.doc:description: Saves the detected MIME type of the uploaded file for use in the IDP request body

try.doc:name: Try Retrieve Access Token
try.doc:description: Attempts to retrieve the Salesforce access token from the Object Store; returns a 401-style error if no token has been stored yet

os:retrieve.doc:name: Retrieve Access Token from Object Store
os:retrieve.doc:description: Reads the Salesforce OAuth2 access token stored under key 'salesforce_access_token' into vars.accessToken

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle Token Not Cached
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Propagates a 401-style error when no Salesforce access token exists in the Object Store; caller must invoke POST /token first

ee:transform.doc:name: Build Token Missing Error
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON 401 error response indicating the caller must obtain a token via POST /token before uploading a file

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the missing token error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON error body prompting the caller to invoke POST /token first

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Unauthorized", message: "No Salesforce access token found in Object Store. Call POST /token first." }

logger.message: Access token retrieved from Object Store. Building IDP request.
logger.doc:name: Log Token Retrieved
logger.doc:description: Logs that the Salesforce access token was successfully retrieved from the Object Store

ee:transform.doc:name: Build IDP Request Body
ee:transform.doc:description: Constructs the JSON request body for the Salesforce Flow IDP extractDataFromDocument action using the Base64 file content and MIME type

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload as the JSON body expected by the Salesforce extractDataFromDocument action

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Wraps fileContent, mimeType, and documentProcessingConfiguration into the inputs array required by the IDP action API

http:request.doc:name: POST to Salesforce Flow IDP
http:request.doc:description: Sends the Base64-encoded file to the Salesforce Flow IDP extractDataFromDocument action using the access token retrieved from the Object Store

ee:transform.doc:name: Parse IDP Response as JSON
ee:transform.doc:description: Converts the Salesforce Flow IDP response into a JSON object containing extractedData fields, confidence scores, and the needsManualReview flag

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload as the parsed JSON representation of the IDP extraction response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Outputs the full IDP response array including actionName, isSuccess, outputValues with extractedData and extractedDataJson

logger.message: #['Flow IDP extraction complete. Success: ' ++ (payload[0].isSuccess default false as String) ++ '. Needs manual review: ' ++ (payload[0].outputValues.extractedData.needsManualReview.value default false as String)]
logger.doc:name: Log IDP Extraction Result
logger.doc:description: Logs the outcome of the Flow IDP document extraction including success status and whether manual review is required

error-handler.doc:description: Handles errors during file extraction and IDP document processing

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle IDP Unauthorized
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles 401 Unauthorized from the Salesforce IDP endpoint; the cached token may be expired — call POST /token to refresh it

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 401 IDP Error Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON 401 error indicating the cached token is expired; caller should refresh via POST /token

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the 401 Unauthorized IDP error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON error body for IDP authorization failures with token expiry guidance

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Unauthorized", message: "Salesforce token is expired or invalid. Call POST /token to refresh the cached access token.", details: error.description }

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle IDP Bad Request
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles 400 Bad Request from the Salesforce IDP endpoint; typically caused by an unsupported MIME type or incorrect IDP config name

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 400 IDP Error Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON 400 error body indicating the IDP request is malformed or the file type is not supported

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the 400 Bad Request IDP error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON error body for IDP bad request failures indicating unsupported file type or incorrect IDP config name

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Bad Request", message: "Salesforce Flow IDP rejected the request. Ensure the file type is PDF, PNG, or JPEG and the IDP config name is correct.", details: error.description }

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle Missing File Part
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles DataWeave expression errors caused by a missing or malformed 'file' part in the multipart request body

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 400 Missing File Error
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON 400 error body explaining that the request must include a multipart file part named 'file'

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the missing file part error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON 400 error body for requests missing the required multipart file part named 'file'

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Bad Request", message: "Request must include a multipart/form-data body with a file part named 'file'.", details: error.description }

on-error-propagate.doc:name: Handle General Error
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Catches any unexpected error during file extraction or IDP processing and returns a structured JSON error response

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 500 Error Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats a JSON 500 error body for unexpected errors during file processing or IDP call

ee:message.doc:description: Defines the output message payload for the 500 Internal Server Error response

ee:set-payload.doc:description: Builds a JSON 500 error body for unexpected failures during file attachment processing or IDP invocation

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Internal Server Error", message: "An unexpected error occurred while processing the file.", details: error.description }
